task.name: Update Income server schema to accept optional and nullable fields
task.action: In `src/app/api/income-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, replace the `ServerPatchIncomeSchema` with one where all fields are `.optional()` and `description` is `.nullable().optional()`. Reference the expense schema at `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts` lines 8-23 for the pattern.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the file compiles with no type errors.

task.name: Rewrite Income PATCH handler to use dynamic updateData
task.action: In the same file, rewrite the PATCH handler's `$transaction` block to: 1. Build an `updateData` object dynamically ŌĆö only include fields present in `parseResult.data` (check `!== undefined`). 2. Handle account changes: if `accountId` is provided AND differs from `existingTransaction.accountId`, restore the old account balance (decrement by oldAmount for income) and apply to the new account (increment by newAmount). 3. Handle amount-only changes on the same account: increment by `balanceDifference`. 4. Handle amount + account change together (same as expense route pattern at lines 197-235). 5. Ensure `onIncomeTransactionChange` is called for both old and new account/date when they change. Reference the expense PATCH handler at lines 158-277 for the full dynamic update pattern.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Then manually test: edit an income transaction's date only (without touching description) ŌĆö should succeed without 400 error.

task.name: Update Transfer server schema to accept optional and nullable fields
task.action: In `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/[id]/route.ts`, replace the `ServerPatchTransferSchema` with one where all fields are `.optional()`, `notes` is `.nullable().optional()`, and `feeAmount` is `.nullable().optional()` (with its refine adjusted). Update the outer `.refine()` for `fromAccountId !== toAccountId` to only run when both fields are provided.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` to confirm the file compiles with no type errors.

task.name: Rewrite Transfer PATCH handler to use dynamic updateData and fix accountsChanged logic
task.action: In the same file, rewrite the PATCH handler to: 1. Build an `updateData` object dynamically ŌĆö only include fields present in `parseResult.data`. 2. Fix the `accountsChanged` variable: change `existingTransfer.fromAccountId === fromAccountId || existingTransfer.toAccountId === toAccountId` to use `!==` so it correctly detects when accounts have actually changed. 3. Preserve the existing fee handling logic (create/update/delete fee expenses) exactly ŌĆö just gate it on the relevant fields being provided. 4. Ensure `onTransferTransactionChange` is called for both old and new states when accounts/dates change. The transfer handler is more complex than income due to fee logic ŌĆö be careful to preserve all fee creation, update, and deletion paths.
task.verify: Run `npm run build`. Then manually test: edit a transfer transaction's date only ŌĆö should succeed without 400 error. Also test editing a transfer with a fee to ensure fee logic still works.

task.name: Run lint and build checks
task.action: Run `npm run lint` and `npm run build`. Fix any lint errors or type errors introduced by the changes.
task.verify: `npm run lint` exits with 0 errors. `npm run build` exits with 0 errors.

task.name: Verify hook error messages are kept
task.action: Confirm that the improved error messages in `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts` (lines 79-82) and `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts` (lines 94-97) are still in place and read the API response body to surface Zod validation details.
task.verify: Read both hook files and confirm the `errorData.details ? JSON.stringify(errorData.details)` pattern is present in the update functions.
